skill.name: api-doc-generator
skill.description: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification documents, request examples, and Postman collections automatically from source code
skill.summary: Transform source code into complete API documentation with OpenAPI 3.0 specs, request samples, and Postman collections
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Two static analysis findings evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alert on SKILL.md:4 is a YAML description field with no cryptography. The 'high file entropy' alert is caused by CJK/Chinese UTF-8 characters, which naturally have higher entropy than ASCII text. No actual security threats found: no network access, no filesystem operations, no external commands, no scripts, no environment variable access, and no prompt injection attempts detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md line 4. This line contains a YAML frontmatter description field ('API文档自动生成 — 代码→OpenAPI 3.0规范文档+请求示例+Postman集合'). No cryptographic algorithm, library, or function call exists in this file. The alert was likely triggered by the CJK characters or arrow symbol being misinterpreted as encoded data.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: High File Entropy
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer reported high file entropy (6.13 bits) as a heuristic indicator of binary or encrypted content. The SKILL.md file contains mixed Chinese (CJK) and ASCII text. Chinese UTF-8 characters use 3 bytes per character, which naturally produces entropy in the 5-8 bit range. The reported 6.13 bits is consistent with normal bilingual text and does not indicate obfuscation, encryption, or binary content.

content.user_title: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 Documentation from Your Source Code
content.value_statement: Writing API documentation by hand is slow and often incomplete. This skill automatically generates OpenAPI 3.0 specifications, request examples, and Postman collections directly from your code so your team always has accurate API docs.
actual_capabilities: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 compliant specification documents from source code
Create request and response examples for each API endpoint
Produce ready-to-import Postman collections for API testing
Document REST endpoints including path parameters, query strings, and headers
Output API documentation in Markdown format for team sharing
Extract API definitions from code written in multiple programming languages
limitations: Cannot execute or test actual API endpoints at runtime
Documentation quality depends on the clarity and completeness of the source code provided
Designed for REST API patterns and does not support GraphQL or gRPC documentation
Generated output requires human review to verify accuracy before publication

use_cases.title: Auto-Generate API Docs from Backend Code
use_cases.description: Backend developers paste their route handlers or controller files and receive complete OpenAPI 3.0 specification documents ready for team use.

use_cases.title: Create Postman Collections for Onboarding
use_cases.description: Frontend developers and new team members get ready-to-import Postman collections with example requests so they can explore and test APIs immediately.

use_cases.title: Standardize API Documentation Across Teams
use_cases.description: Engineering leads use this skill to enforce consistent OpenAPI documentation across microservices, ensuring every API follows the same specification format.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Endpoint Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate API documentation for this Express route handler. Include the HTTP method, path, request parameters, and response format.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer has a simple route file and wants quick documentation

prompt_templates.title: OpenAPI 3.0 Full Specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete OpenAPI 3.0 YAML specification for this REST API controller. Include all endpoints, request schemas, response schemas, and error codes.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer needs a standards-compliant OpenAPI spec for their API gateway

prompt_templates.title: Postman Collection with Auth Headers
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Postman collection JSON from this API definition. Include authorization headers, example request bodies, and test scripts for each endpoint.
prompt_templates.scenario: A QA engineer needs a ready-to-use Postman collection for API testing

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Language API Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract API definitions from these source files written in Python, Java, and TypeScript. Generate a unified OpenAPI 3.0 specification with consistent naming and examples.
prompt_templates.scenario: An architect needs to document APIs across a polyglot microservice architecture

output_examples.input: Express route: GET /users/:id returns { id, name, email } with status 200 or 404
output_examples.output: OpenAPI 3.0 YAML documenting GET /users/{id} with path parameter schema, successful response schema including id (integer), name (string), email (string), and 404 error response definition

output_examples.input: POST /orders endpoint accepting { productId, quantity, shippingAddress } and returning { orderId, status, total }
output_examples.output: Complete request body schema with required fields, response schema with order confirmation details, and a ready-to-use Postman collection entry with sample JSON payload

output_examples.input: Refactor this internal API documentation into a public-facing OpenAPI reference
output_examples.output: Cleaned OpenAPI specification with security scheme definitions, rate-limiting documentation, versioning headers, and Markdown-formatted endpoint descriptions suitable for a public developer portal

best_practices: Provide complete source code files with all routes and handlers for the most comprehensive documentation output
Always review generated OpenAPI specs for correctness and add any business logic details the code alone cannot express
Include sample request payloads and expected response data in your prompt to produce richer API documentation
anti_patterns: Do not paste source code containing API keys, tokens, or secrets when requesting documentation generation
Do not publish generated API documentation without first reviewing and correcting endpoint descriptions and schemas
Avoid documenting internal-only or deprecated endpoints that should not be exposed to external API consumers

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill can read and interpret API definitions from most common backend languages including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, Ruby, and PHP.

faq.question: Does this skill generate Swagger 2.0 or OpenAPI 3.0?
faq.answer: It generates OpenAPI 3.0 specifications, which is the current standard. OpenAPI 3.0 is the successor to Swagger 2.0 and includes improved schema definitions.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated Postman collection?
faq.answer: Yes, you can describe any customizations such as authentication headers, environment variables, or test scripts in your prompt and they will be included.

faq.question: Does this skill require network access to my API?
faq.answer: No, the skill generates documentation purely from source code analysis. It does not connect to your API or make any network requests.

faq.question: How is this different from tools like Swagger UI?
faq.answer: This skill generates the specification file itself from raw code. Swagger UI renders existing specs. Use this skill to create the spec, then tools like Swagger UI to display it.

faq.question: Where can I use this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex. It can be used directly in your coding environment or through the AI assistant interface.
